perturbation.original_text: Section 7.1 Promotion Fees. (a) In consideration for King's performance of its obligations under this Agreement, Depomed shall pay promotion fees (the "Promotion Fees") to King as follows: following each Agreement Quarter during the Term, Depomed shall pay to King 50% of the Gross Margin for such Agreement Quarter.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 7.1 Promotion Fees. (a) In consideration for King's performance of its obligations under this Agreement, Depomed shall pay promotion fees (the "Promotion Fees") to King as follows: following each Agreement Quarter during the Term, Depomed shall pay to King 50% of the Gross Margin for the first two years, and then 75% of the Gross Margin thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction because Section 7.1(a) now specifies a different percentage for calculating promotion fees based on the length of the agreement. This directly conflicts with the original clause which states a static 50% Gross Margin throughout the agreement. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty on which calculation is to be used.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
Section 7.1 Promotion Fees. (a) In consideration for King's performance of its obligations under this Agreement, Depomed shall pay promotion fees (the "Promotion Fees") to King as follows: following each Agreement Quarter during the Term, Depomed shall pay to King 50% of the Gross Margin for the first two years, and then 75% of the Gross Margin thereafter.
(b) Within thirty (30) days following the end of each Agreement Quarter during the Term, Depomed shall provide King with a statement setting forth: (i) the aggregate number of Units of Product sold to customers in the Territory during such Agreement Quarter; (ii) Net Sales during such Agreement Quarter; (iii) Depomed Net Sales during such Agreement Quarter (if any); (iv) COGS during such Agreement Quarter (based on Depomed's Standard Cost); (v) Advertising/Marketing/Educational Expense with respect to the costs of Samples (based on Depomed's Standard Cost) during such Agreement Quarter; (vi) Gross Margin for such Agreement Quarter; and (vii) a calculation of the amount, if any, payable by Depomed to King in respect of such Agreement Quarter pursuant to Section 7.3(a).
perturbation.justification: The changed text specifies different percentages for calculating promotion fees based on the length of the agreement (50% for the first two years, then 75% thereafter). This directly conflicts with the original clause, and now also the contradicted text, which implies a static fee structure, creating a clear inconsistency as different sections of a contract now provide conflicting payment obligations. This creates in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations, matching the 'Inconsistencies' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Section 8.1 Term. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue, unless terminated sooner in accordance with this Article VIII, until June 27, 2011 (the "Term"). The Term of this Agreement shall be extended for subsequent one year periods upon the mutual agreement of the parties, which agreement shall be set forth in writing (in which event a party that desires to so extend the Term of this Agreement shall notify the other party at least 120 days prior to the termination of this Agreement).
perturbation.changed_text: Section 8.1 Term. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue, unless terminated sooner in accordance with this Article VIII, until June 27, 2011 (the "Term"). The Term of this Agreement shall be extended for subsequent two year periods upon the mutual agreement of the parties, which agreement can be an oral one (in which event a party that desires to so extend the Term of this Agreement shall notify the other party at least 60 days prior to the termination of this Agreement).
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction because Section 8.1 now allows for oral agreements to extend the term and shortens the notification period. This directly conflicts with the original clause which requires written agreements and a longer notification period. This creates ambiguity regarding the proper procedure for extending the contract, potentially leading to disputes over its validity after the initial term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 16.4 Amendments. This Agreement may not be amended, modified, altered, or supplemented except by means of a written agreement or other instrument executed by both of the parties hereto. No course of conduct or dealing between the parties will act as a modification or waiver of any provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 8.1 now permits oral agreements for extending the contract term and shortens the notification period to 60 days. This directly contradicts Section 16.4, which mandates that all amendments or modifications to the agreement must be in writing. The introduction of oral agreements in Section 8.1 clashes with the explicit requirement of written agreements in Section 16.4, creating a significant contradiction regarding how the contract can be modified. The reduced notification period also impacts the amendment procedure.

perturbation.original_text: Section 11.1 Indemnification. Each party will defend, at its own expense, indemnify and hold harmless the other party and its Affiliates from and against any and all damages, liabilities, losses, costs, and expenses, including reasonable attorneys' fees, arising out of any Third Party claim, suit or proceeding brought against the other party or its Affiliates to the extent such claim, suit, or proceeding is based upon a claim arising out of or relating to (i) any breach or violation of, or failure to perform, any covenant or agreement made by such indemnifying party in this Agreement, unless waived in writing by the indemnified party; (ii) any breach of the representations or warranties made by such indemnifying party in this Agreement; or (iii) the negligence or willful misconduct of the indemnifying party, except (under any of (i) or (ii)) to the extent arising out of the breach, violation, failure, negligence or willful misconduct of the indemnified party.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 11.1 Indemnification. Each party will defend, at its own expense, indemnify and hold harmless the other party and its Affiliates from and against any and all damages, liabilities, losses, costs, and expenses, including reasonable attorneys' fees, arising out of any Third Party claim, suit or proceeding brought against the other party or its Affiliates to the extent such claim, suit, or proceeding is based upon a claim arising out of or relating to (i) any breach or violation of, or failure to perform, any covenant or agreement made by such indemnifying party in this Agreement, unless waived orally by the indemnified party; (ii) any breach of the representations or warranties made by such indemnifying party in this Agreement; or (iii) the negligence or willful misconduct of the indemnifying party, except (under any of (i) or (ii)) to the extent arising out of the breach, violation, failure, negligence or willful misconduct of the indemnified party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction because Section 11.1 now allows for oral waivers for breaches. This directly conflicts with the original clause which requires written waivers. This creates ambiguity regarding the validity of waivers, potentially leading to disputes over indemnification obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 16.4 Amendments. This Agreement may not be amended, modified, altered, or supplemented except by means of a written agreement or other instrument executed by both of the parties hereto. No course of conduct or dealing between the parties will act as a modification or waiver of any provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 11.1 allows for oral waivers, directly contradicting Section 16.4, which explicitly states that amendments, modifications, or waivers must be in writing. This creates a clear and direct conflict, making the contract internally inconsistent and potentially unenforceable as it is now unclear whether oral waivers are valid.
